title: Privacy, Bias, and Ethics in Face Analysis
concept: Deploying face analysis systems requires understanding privacy concerns, demographic bias, consent, and security implications.

exercises.prompt: Recap: Why is face alignment important?

options.text: It normalizes rotation and scale, making input consistent for recognition.
It decreases the speed of the pipeline.

exercises.explain: Correct! Face alignment straightens and standardizes the crop, which is crucial for high recognition accuracy.

cards.title: A powerful technology
cards.body: Because faces are unique and public, facial analysis is incredibly powerful. But with great power comes great responsibility. If a face recognition model makes a mistake, someone could be locked out of their account, or worse, falsely accused of a crime.

cards.title: Key Ethical Challenges
cards.body:
- Demographic Bias: Models trained on imbalanced datasets perform poorly on minority populations.
- Privacy & Consent: Collecting faces without permission violates privacy rights.
- Security & Misuse: Face databases can be targeted by hackers.

cards.title: The biased witness
cards.body: Imagine a witness who has only ever lived in a small town. When asked to identify people from a diverse city, they struggle because they aren't familiar with different facial features. A neural network behaves the same way if its training set lacks diversity.

cards.title: Demographic bias in practice
cards.body: Studies have shown that some commercial face recognition systems have significantly higher error rates (higher False Match and False Non-Match rates) for female and darker-skinned faces due to imbalanced training data.

cards.title: Models are not 100% objective
cards.body: You might think a math model cannot be biased. But neural networks learn from data. If the data is collected with historical bias, or if it lacks diversity, the model's predictions will inherit and reinforce those biases.

cards.title: Mitigating Bias
cards.body: How engineers work to build ethical face models:

left.text:
Imbalanced training dataset
High error rate on minorities
Lacks transparency

right.text:
Diverse, representative dataset
Regular audits for bias
Secure, encrypted vectors

exercises.prompt: What is the primary cause of demographic bias in face recognition models?

options.text: Training models on imbalanced datasets that lack diversity
Using the cosine similarity metric instead of L2 distance
Running models on mobile devices instead of GPUs

exercises.explain: Bias is primarily caused by unrepresentative training data that fails to capture the diversity of human faces.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Since algorithms run on mathematical equations, they are automatically free from human bias.
exercises.explain: False. Models learn directly from training data. If the data is biased or imbalanced, the model's outputs will be biased as well.

exercises.sentence: To ensure fairness, engineers must train face models on ___ datasets.
exercises.explain: A diverse and representative training dataset is essential to reduce demographic bias and ensure fair predictions.

exercises.prompt: Which of these practices represents an ethical approach to deploying face recognition?

options.text: Auditing the model across various age and ethnic groups to verify equal accuracy before release
Scraping social media profiles without user consent to build a database
Hiding system error rates to avoid public concern

exercises.explain: Regularly auditing models for demographic fairness is a core component of ethical AI development.

exercises.prompt: You are deploying a security system at a corporate office. During testing, you find the face recognition model works perfectly for male employees but frequently rejects female employees. What should you do?

options.text: Audit the training data for demographic balance, collect more diverse sample photos, and retrain the model.
Disable the verification step entirely for all male employees.
Lower the matching threshold for everyone to 0.1, ignoring security risks.
Blame the camera sensors and buy more expensive lenses.

exercises.explain: Exactly! Unbalanced accuracy indicates demographic bias, typically caused by imbalanced training data. Auditing and diversifying the dataset is the correct approach. Fantastic work! You have finished the first unit on Face Detection and Recognition. Next up: Facial Recognition & Verification Pipelines!
